---
title: "Multi-Modal Level of Service (MMLoS)"
description-meta: "Research project developing a multi-modal level-of-service measurement framework and tool for Active Travel England"
image: "files/images/mmlos-hero.svg"
page-layout: custom
---

::: {.landing-shell}

::: {.landing-profile .landing-panel}
<div class="landing-logo">
![](files/images/mmlos-logo.svg){width="120px"}
</div>

## MMLoS – Multi-Modal Level of Service {.landing-title}

<div class="landing-tagline">
Measuring street quality across **walking, cycling, public transport, and driving** in a single, comparable framework.
</div>

::: {.landing-actions}
[**Learn About the Project**]{.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}[About]{.button}
[**View Team**]{.btn .btn-outline .btn-lg}[Team]{.button}
[**Read the Review**]{.btn .btn-outline .btn-lg}[Research]{.button}
:::

<div class="landing-status">
🚧 **In Progress** — Research phase (Apr 2026 – Sep 2027) · Funded by Active Travel England
</div>
:::

::: {.landing-grid}

::: {.landing-card .landing-panel}
### 🎯 Project Goal

Develop a robust, evidence-based **Multi-Modal Level of Service (MMLoS) tool** that enables policymakers and practitioners to evaluate how streets support sustainable and active transport modes—walking, cycling, public transit—in a **commensurable** way alongside motor traffic.

Key output: a working proof-of-concept web application usable by local authorities and Active Travel England.
:::

::: {.landing-card .landing-panel}
### 🔍 Why MMLoS Matters

Current appraisal tools (like the Highway Capacity Manual's LOS or ATE's checklists) either:

- Focus narrowly on **vehicle throughput** (traditional LOS), or
- Use **checklist-style scoring** that doesn't produce a continuous, comparable measure

MMLoS fills the gap: a **single numerical score** (or set of scores) that meaningfully compares quality across modes, exposing trade-offs and guiding balanced investment.
:::

::: {.landing-card .landing-panel}
### 🛠️ Approach

We are combining:

1. **Literature synthesis** — reviewing US FDOT/NCHRP methods, UK WebTAG, European practice
2. **Data innovation** — leveraging OpenStreetMap, street imagery, and potentially citizen science data
3. **Case study validation** — Leeds, Essex, West Midlands (three diverse contexts)
4. **Tool development** — web app + open-source implementation

The project is a collaboration between **University of Westminster (Active Travel Academy)** and **University of Leeds (Institute for Transport Studies)**, advised by a Steering Group including local authority partners.
:::

::: {.landing-card .landing-panel}
### 🔗 Related Content

- **Literature review** — Comprehensive review of existing MMLoS frameworks, gaps, and emerging technologies (TRELLIS image-to-3D, BetaStreets citizen science, OSM quality)
- **Bid Documentation** — Full research proposal (February 2026)
- **GitHub Organisation** — Code, data, and methods: [multilevelos](https://github.com/multilevelos)
:::

:::

:::
